items.text:
NOTICE: Scheduled Water Shutoff

Westbrook Hall and Stanton Hall will undergo a scheduled water shutoff for pipe maintenance from Monday, June 8 to Wednesday, June 10, between 8:00 AM and 4:00 PM each day.

During the shutoff window, please observe the following:
• Bathrooms and kitchenettes on floors 2-6 will be unavailable.
• Bottled water stations will be set up in the main lobby of each building from 7:30 AM onward.
• Residents who require accessible facilities should report to the Henderson Center, where a $5 daily shower pass will be waived for affected students.
• Outside food and drink containers are not permitted in study rooms during this period.

All affected residents will receive a confirmation email by Friday, June 5. For maintenance questions, contact the Facilities Office at extension 4221 or visit Room 108 between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM on weekdays.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will happen on the lower floors during the shutoff?

options.A: Residents on those floors will be charged a daily fee.
options.B: Bathrooms on floors 2 through 6 will be unavailable.
options.C: Lobby access will be restricted from 8:00 AM onward.
options.D: All study rooms in both buildings will close for repairs.

questions.explanation: The notice states that "bathrooms and kitchenettes on floors 2-6 will be unavailable" during the shutoff window.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who need accessible facilities?

options.A: They are expected to remain in their dorm rooms during the shutoff.
options.B: They will be reassigned to a different residence hall for the week.
options.C: They can use the Henderson Center without paying the usual fee.
options.D: They must submit a written request five business days in advance.

questions.explanation: The notice says affected students with accessible needs go to the Henderson Center, where the "$5 daily shower pass will be waived" for them.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To inform residents about a planned water shutoff and alternative arrangements.
options.B: To recruit student volunteers to staff the bottled water stations during maintenance.
options.C: To explain the long-term plumbing renovation plan across all residence halls.
options.D: To announce a new permanent fee structure for accessible shower facilities.

questions.explanation: The notice describes the upcoming shutoff dates and tells residents what alternatives — bottled water, the Henderson Center, contact information — are available, which matches A.

items.text:
Campus Shuttle Service: Summer Timetable Changes (Effective June 1)

Route A — North Quad ↔ Medical Campus
• Monday-Friday: 7:00 AM to 10:00 PM, every 15 minutes
• Saturday: 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, every 30 minutes
• Sunday: Service suspended

Route B — South Lot ↔ Central Library
• Monday-Friday: 7:30 AM to 8:00 PM, every 20 minutes
• Saturday: 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM, every 40 minutes
• Sunday: Service suspended

All riders must tap a current student ID at boarding. The $45 summer pass is required for any rider boarding more than ten times per month. Single-ride passengers may pay $2.00 in exact change to the driver.

Note on late arrivals: shuttles will hold at each stop for no longer than two minutes. Riders who miss the connection should consult the next departure displayed on the digital board.

For lost items, contact the Transit Office at extension 5512 between 8:00 AM and 5:00 PM. Service updates and weather suspensions are posted on the student portal each morning by 6:30 AM.

questions.stem: According to the schedule, when does Route B run on Saturday?

options.A: From 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM, every fifteen minutes.
options.B: From 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM, every forty minutes.
options.C: From 9:00 AM to 8:00 PM, every twenty minutes.
options.D: From 8:00 AM to 10:00 PM, every thirty minutes.

questions.explanation: The schedule lists Route B Saturday service as "10:00 AM to 4:00 PM, every 40 minutes."

questions.stem: The word "suspended" in the timetable is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: Sunday "service suspended" indicates that no shuttle runs that day — service is stopped, not increased, extended, or rerouted.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a rider who boards a shuttle twelve times in July?

options.A: The rider must pay $2.00 in exact change at each boarding.
options.B: The rider can ride for free with any current student ID.
options.C: The rider will be required to buy the $45 summer pass.
options.D: The rider can only use Route A during weekday hours.

questions.explanation: The schedule states the $45 summer pass "is required for any rider boarding more than ten times per month," so twelve trips triggers the pass requirement.
